title: Illustrative Unified Analysis determinism calibration
researchQuestion: Does repeated Unified Analysis of the same frozen input produce structurally identical findings and metrics under a controlled deterministic environment?

hypothesis.statement: Repeated Unified Analysis produces structurally identical findings and metrics for the same frozen input and environment.
hypothesis.nullHypothesis: At least one repeated Unified Analysis result differs structurally under the same frozen input and environment.
hypothesis.rationale: The current analysis pipeline defines deterministic finding ordering, but this illustrative experiment has not been executed.

motivation: Calibrate the experiment contract using an existing QAIP analysis capability without claiming a completed experiment.

included: Unified Analysis Validation and Coverage outputs
excluded: Performance and scientific validity of coverage metrics

independent.name: Execution repetition
independent.measurementOrControl: Enumerated from 1 to the planned repeat count

dependent.name: Structural output identity
dependent.definition: Equality of normalized findings and metrics
dependent.measurementOrControl: Comparison method remains to be frozen

controlled.name: Input model
controlled.measurementOrControl: Dataset reference will be frozen before READY

assumptions.statement: The analysis input can be frozen.

threatsToValidity.description: Structural identity may not imply semantic equivalence.
threatsToValidity.mitigation: Limit the conclusion to structural identity.

considerations.security: No secrets may be included in inputs or artifacts.
considerations.confidentiality: Only repository-owned illustrative data is planned.
